steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The conclusion hinges on a contrast based on temperature change: higher surface temperatures increase the percentage of sites where thermal pollution can meet home heating needs. The strongest clue is the shift in effect (more sites becoming viable) under the maximum plausible temperature scenario.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice D shows that under maximum temperatures, the portion of sites unable to use thermal pollution drops below 20%, which directly supports the conclusion (since if 80% of sites can now meet needs, the percentage of viable sites has increased). A, B, and C either misrepresent the direction of change or fail to show a meaningful increase in viable sites. That matches choice D — D is correct.
